posts.name: Alexandre Nikitenko
posts.text:
Dear experts,
Nick recommend to constraint background rate within some uncertainty
using lines
bkgrate_sr3_17 rateParam  SR3_2017 bkg 1
bkgrate_sr3_17 param 1 0.025
meaning that I want background to float within 2.5 % around the nominal value
I defined in datacards (1850 events).
The question is the following:
does it assume flat distribution for bkg rate or Gaussian with sigma 2.5 % or simply does not allow bkg rate be outside 2.5%  variation in the fit ?
Thanks, Sasha.
posts.cooked_html:
Dear experts,

Nick recommend to constraint background rate within some uncertainty
using lines

bkgrate_sr3_17 rateParam SR3_2017 bkg 1
bkgrate_sr3_17 param 1 0.025

meaning that I want background to float within 2.5 % around the nominal value
I defined in datacards (1850 events).

The question is the following:

does it assume flat distribution for bkg rate or Gaussian with sigma 2.5 % or simply does not allow bkg rate be outside 2.5% variation in the fit ?

Thanks, Sasha.

posts.name: Andrea Carlo Marini
posts.text:
Hi Sasha,
Both the lines:
bkgrate_sr3_17 rateParam SR3_2017 bkg 1
bkgrate_sr3_17 param 1 0.025

will add a Gaussian constarin to the background of +/- 2.5%
Only the first line:
bkgrate_sr3_17 rateParam SR3_2017 bkg 1

will add a free uncontrained parameter  to the background.
-A
posts.cooked_html:
Hi Sasha,

Both the lines:

bkgrate_sr3_17 rateParam SR3_2017 bkg 1
bkgrate_sr3_17 param 1 0.025

will add a Gaussian constarin to the background of +/- 2.5%

Only the first line:

bkgrate_sr3_17 rateParam SR3_2017 bkg 1

will add a free uncontrained parameter to the background.

-A

posts.name: Nicholas Wardle
posts.text:
As Andrea said,
The line you have will create a Gaussian constraint.
I just want to clarify that I didn’t recommend adding any constraint, I just said how to add one of it’s needed.
Nick
posts.cooked_html:
As Andrea said,

The line you have will create a Gaussian constraint.

I just want to clarify that I didn’t recommend adding any constraint, I just said how to add one of it’s needed.

Nick

posts.name: Alexandre Nikitenko
posts.text:
Thanks Andrea, Nick !
Nick, no worry, I did not say that you recommend to use a constraint.
We do want to have it from ourside.
Cheers, Sasha
